title: Zero-Day Exploit Attempt - Memory Corruption in Public-Facing Web Application
raw_evidence: 2025-07-14T04:51:17Z — WAF alert: 14 requests from 91.108.56.144 targeting /api/v2/upload endpoint with malformed multipart MIME boundary strings — payload length exceeds parser buffer by 4096 bytes.
2025-07-14T04:51:19Z — Web application process (node_app, PID 1882) crashed and was auto-restarted by systemd — core dump captured.
2025-07-14T04:51:22Z — Second crash 3 seconds later — attacker adjusting payload. Third request did not crash the process.
2025-07-14T04:51:28Z — Post-exploitation indicator: /tmp/.x (hidden executable, 47KB, ELF binary) written to disk by node_app process — node_app should never write executables.
2025-07-14T04:51:31Z — /tmp/.x executed by node_app — spawned outbound connection to 91.108.56.144:9001 (reverse shell).
2025-07-14T04:51:35Z — CVE cross-reference: payload structure matches PoC for CVE-2025-XXXX (unpatched — vendor patch released 18 hours ago, not yet applied).
2025-07-14T04:52:00Z — Reverse shell active for 43 seconds before network egress block triggered. Commands observed: id, uname -a, cat /etc/passwd.

context.prior_alert_detail: Port scan from 91.108.56.144 detected 2025-07-13T22:14Z — no action taken.
context.patch_status: CVE-2025-XXXX patch available 18h ago — not yet applied per change management schedule
context.asset_criticality_destination: CRITICAL — public-facing production web application; customer data in scope
